samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelined processing. Implement sequential logic for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation. Ensure proper synchronization and data flow through the pipeline stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal for chip reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 3'b000.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD = 3'b001.</state>
        <state id="CALC">State CALC = 3'b010.</state>
        <state id="OUT">State OUT = 3'b011.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand input.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier input.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product output.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                mul_en_out_reg <= 3'b0;
                mul_a_reg <= 8'b0;
                mul_b_reg <= 8'b0;
                temp[0] <= 16'b0;
                temp[1] <= 16'b0;
                temp[2] <= 16'b0;
                temp[3] <= 16'b0;
                temp[4] <= 16'b0;
                temp[5] <= 16'b0;
                temp[6] <= 16'b0;
                temp[7] <= 16'b0;
                sum[0] <= 16'b0;
                sum[1] <= 16'b0;
                sum[2] <= 16'b0;
                sum[3] <= 16'b0;
                sum[4] <= 16'b0;
                sum[5] <= 16'b0;
                mul_out_reg <= 16'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update the output enable register with the input enable signal
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[1:0], mul_en_in};
                
                // Update the multiplicand register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[2]) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                end
                
                // Update the multiplier register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[2]) begin
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
                
                // Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits
                temp[0] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[0]}}, mul_a_reg} << 0;
                temp[1] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[1]}}, mul_a_reg} << 1;
                temp[2] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[2]}}, mul_a_reg} << 2;
                temp[3] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[3]}}, mul_a_reg} << 3;
                temp[4] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[4]}}, mul_a_reg} << 4;
                temp[5] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[5]}}, mul_a_reg} << 5;
                temp[6] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[6]}}, mul_a_reg} << 6;
                temp[7] <= {{8{mul_b_reg[7]}}, mul_a_reg} << 7;
                
                // Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6];
                sum[4] <= temp[7];
                sum[5] <= sum[1] + sum[2];
                sum[6] <= sum[3] + sum[4];
                sum[7] <= sum[5] + sum[6];
                
                // Accumulate the final product
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[7];
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic blocks for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation.</implementation>
</module>
